meta.section: Endpoint Management — Windows (Phase 3 of 7: Endpoint Security — Account Protection / Firewall / Disk Encryption)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune) — portal paths, profile types, and BitLocker/LAPS settings grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: All five are Endpoint security policies (Endpoint security node): Account protection (LAPS + Local user group membership profiles), Firewall, and Disk encryption. Pattern for each: Create Policy > Platform = Windows > pick the named Profile > configure > assign.

LAPS prerequisite: the tenant-level 'Enable Microsoft Entra LAPS' toggle must already be On (Entra ID > Devices > Device settings — that's the LocalAdministratorPassword item from the Authentication Methods section). Assign LAPS to DEVICE groups, not user groups, to avoid config conflicts.
Assign to Windows device groups and pilot the two disruptive ones (Local Admins, BitLocker) before broad rollout.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Endpoint security > Account protection > profile: Local admin password solution (Windows LAPS)

requiredSettings.label: Backup Directory
requiredSettings.value: Backup the password to Microsoft Entra ID

requiredSettings.label: Password requirements
requiredSettings.value: Length/complexity per baseline; Password Age (rotation) e.g. 30 days

steps: Confirm the tenant LAPS toggle is On (Entra ID > Devices > Device settings).
Intune admin center > Endpoint security > Account protection > Create Policy > Windows > profile Local admin password solution (Windows LAPS).
Set Backup Directory = Microsoft Entra ID; set password length/complexity and the rotation age.
Assign to a Windows DEVICE group (not user group). Note: LOW impact / invisible to users — recover/rotate passwords from Devices > (device) > Local admin password recovery.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Endpoint security > Account protection > Windows LAPS profile (account-management settings)

requiredSettings.label: Administrator Account Name
requiredSettings.value: Target the managed local admin account (blank = the built-in RID-500 account)

requiredSettings.label: Automatic account management
requiredSettings.value: Per baseline — manage/enable/rename the local admin account and post-authentication actions (reset password after use)

steps: Same Windows LAPS profile as above (Endpoint security > Account protection) — this row covers its account-management / access-control settings.
Set Administrator Account Name to the managed account (or leave blank for the built-in local admin), and configure automatic account management / post-authentication reset per the baseline.
Assign to the Windows device group. Note: LOW impact / IT-workflow only — pairs with the LAPS row above; keep them in one coherent LAPS policy per device to avoid CSP conflicts.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Endpoint security > Account protection > profile: Local user group membership

requiredSettings.label: Action
requiredSettings.value: Replace/Restrict members to a defined set (e.g. an Entra admin group + the LAPS-managed account) — removes ad-hoc local admin rights from standard users

steps: Intune admin center > Endpoint security > Account protection > Create Policy > Windows > profile Local user group membership.
Target the Administrators group and set the action (Add/Remove/Replace) so membership is limited to the approved accounts only.
Pilot first, then assign to the Windows device group.

Note (HIGH impact): users who currently have local admin are stripped of it — they can no longer install software or change system settings freely. This is one of the most disruptive policies; communicate and provide a support path (LAPS / elevation process) before enforcing.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Endpoint security > Firewall > profile: Microsoft Defender Firewall

requiredSettings.label: Enable firewall
requiredSettings.value: Enabled for all three network profiles — Domain, Private, and Public

steps: Intune admin center > Endpoint security > Firewall > Create Policy > Windows > profile Microsoft Defender Firewall.
Turn the firewall On (true) for the Domain, Private, and Public profiles; keep the baseline inbound/outbound defaults.
Assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — invisible to most users; only environments with unusual local firewall rules might see connection differences.

requiredSettings.label: Policy type
requiredSettings.value: Endpoint security > Disk encryption > profile: BitLocker

requiredSettings.label: Allow Warning For Other Disk Encryption
requiredSettings.value: Disabled (enables silent encryption — verify NO third-party encryption software is present first)

requiredSettings.label: Allow Standard User Encryption
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (so encryption runs for non-admin users)

requiredSettings.label: Recovery
requiredSettings.value: Require device to back up recovery information to Microsoft Entra = Yes

steps: First confirm devices have no third-party disk-encryption software (silent BitLocker can conflict) and support TPM.
Intune admin center > Endpoint security > Disk encryption > Create Policy > Windows > profile BitLocker.
Set Require Device Encryption = Enabled; Allow Warning For Other Disk Encryption = Disabled; Allow Standard User Encryption = Enabled; Require backup of recovery info to Microsoft Entra = Yes.

Assign to the Windows device group. Note (MEDIUM impact): unencrypted devices silently begin BitLocker (one-time encryption pass, temporary perf hit); recovery keys escrow to Entra. Older hardware without a compatible TPM may fail to encrypt and show non-compliant — pairs with the Encryption compliance policy (Phase 1).
